column_metadata.name: object_type
column_metadata.comment:
-- FieldIsFilteringAllowed: True
  -- FieldOuroborosTypeDeclaration: char(4)
  -- description: Type of the Training Course Catalog Entry.
  -- displayName: Training Type Code
  -- synonyms: ['lo_type', 'learning_object_type', 'course_type']
  -- sampleValues: ['OLO', 'ILT', 'TST', 'CUR', 'VID', 'MAT', 'QK', 'SVY', 'PRG']
  -- businessMeaning: The short code identifying the type of learning object. This is the most-used filter in CSOD catalog reports — it determines what kind of training a row represents. OLO = Online Course (self-paced e-learning); ILT = Instructor-Led Training (classroom or VILT); TST = Test/Assessment; CUR = Curriculum (structured course bundle); VID = Video; MAT = Material/Document; QK = Quick Course; SVY = Survey; PRG = Program. Always filter by object_type when building type-specific reports to avoid mixing course types that have different completion mechanics.
  -- enumValues: OLO = Online Course; ILT = Instructor-Led Training; TST = Test; CUR = Curriculum; VID = Video; MAT = Material; QK = Quick Course; SVY = Survey; PRG = Program; PST = Postal Mail
  -- valueFormat: Text, 4-character code
  -- isPII: False
  -- isSensitive: False
  -- tags: ['catalog', 'training_type', 'filtering', 'core']
  -- datatype: nvarchar(4)

properties.enumValues: OLO = Online Course; ILT = Instructor-Led Training; TST = Test; CUR = Curriculum; VID = Video; MAT = Material; QK = Quick Course; SVY = Survey; PRG = Program; PST = Postal Mail
properties.description: Type of the Training Course Catalog Entry.
properties.businessMeaning: The short code identifying the type of learning object. This is the most-used filter in CSOD catalog reports — it determines what kind of training a row represents. csod_kpi_recommendations.name: Training Completion Rate
csod_kpi_recommendations.description: Percent of assigned learners who completed required training in the period. Business value: primary measure of learning effectiveness and compliance.

csod_kpi_recommendations.name: MoM Completion Change (%)
csod_kpi_recommendations.description: Month-over-month percent change in the completion rate. Business value: tracks momentum and the impact of interventions.

csod_kpi_recommendations.name: On-Time Completion Rate
csod_kpi_recommendations.description: Share of completions occurring within the configured due window. Business value: indicates training timeliness and potential compliance risk.

csod_kpi_recommendations.name: Overdue Training Count
csod_kpi_recommendations.description: Number of overdue training assignments. Business value: identifies risk areas requiring escalation.

csod_kpi_recommendations.name: ILT Attendance Rate
csod_kpi_recommendations.description: Percent of ILT sessions with recorded attendance relative to scheduled sessions. Business value: measures ILT effectiveness and capacity utilization.

csod_table_recommendations.reason: Required as the primary anchor for assignment-level metrics (completed_count, monthly_training_completion_rate, in_progress_count). Columns used for joins: user_id, object_id, _last_touched_dt_utc, reg_num.

csod_table_recommendations.description: Primary assignment record table. Contains assignment metadata and learner identifiers used to compute completions, in-progress status, and the assignment timestamp.

csod_table_recommendations.reason: Used to identify actual attendance/completion events (is_attended, create_dt) and to calculate monthly completion rates and ILT session counts.

csod_metric_recommendations.name: Completed Trainings Count
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Raw count of completed training assignment records (useful as the numerator for completion-rate calculations).
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: How many completed training assignment records do we have in transcript_src_assignment_core and training_part_attendance_core over the last 12 months?

csod_metric_recommendations.name: Month-over-Month Training Completion Rate
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Proportion of assigned learners who completed training each calendar month. Primary metric for the requested month-over-month view (last 12 months).
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is our month-over-month training completion rate based on transcript_src_assignment_core.user_id and training_part_attendance_core.is_attended for the last 12 months?

csod_metric_recommendations.name: MoM Training Completion % Change
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Month-over-month percent change in the training completion rate to highlight momentum or deterioration.
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is the percent change in training completion rate month over month using transcript_src_assignment_core and training_part_attendance_core?

csod_metric_recommendations.name: On-Time Completion Rate
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Share of completions that occurred before or within the training's due window (uses training_model_training_object_core due_date_duration as the configured due window anchor).
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What percent of completions occurred on time using transcript_src_assignment_core._last_touched_dt_utc, training_part_attendance_core.create_dt and training_model_training_object_core.due_date_duration?

csod_metric_recommendations.name: Overdue Trainings Count
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Count of assigned training instances considered overdue relative to their configured due window (useful to prioritize reminders/manager escalation).
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: How many training assignments are overdue based on transcript_src_assignment_core._last_touched_dt_utc and training_model_training_object_core.due_date_duration?

csod_metric_recommendations.name: In-Progress Trainings Count
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Count of assignment records currently marked as in-progress (useful as a leading indicator for future completions).
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: How many assignments are currently in progress using transcript_src_assignment_core.is_progress_maintained and transcript_src_assignment_core._last_touched_dt_utc?

csod_metric_recommendations.name: ILT Sessions Conducted
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Count of instructor-led training sessions where attendance was recorded (helps normalize completion counts against live sessions).
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: How many ILT sessions were conducted using training_part_attendance_core.is_attended and training_part_attendance_core.create_dt?

csod_metric_recommendations.name: Attendance-to-Assignment Rate
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Share of assigned learners who have attended at least one session or recorded an attended completion (connects attendance to assignment records).
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What percent of assigned learners have attendance recorded (training_part_attendance_core.is_attended) relative to assignments in transcript_src_assignment_core?

csod_metric_recommendations.name: Training Completion Count by Course
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Monthly completions broken down by course (transcript_src_assignment_core.object_id) to identify which courses drive overall completion rate changes.
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is the monthly count of completions by course using transcript_src_assignment_core.object_id and training_part_attendance_core.is_attended?

csod_metric_recommendations.name: Funnel Certified Count (terminal)
csod_metric_recommendations.description: Monthly count of learners who reached certification from their assigned funnel cohort — identified as a terminal outcome in the causal graph and useful as a business outcome aligned to completion.
csod_metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is the monthly funnel_certified_count using transcript_src_assignment_core.user_id and training_part_attendance_core.is_attended?

presented.message: I understand you're asking about Show me the  month over month training completion rates for my organization in the last 1 year. I've identified 2 relevant analysis area(s). Which one should I focus on?

options.label: Transcript & Statuses

presented.message: Based on your question, here's the analysis area I found. Confirm to proceed or rephrase if this isn't what you meant:

options.label: Learning Completion & Status
options.description: Compute month-over-month training completion rates for the entire organization for the past 12 months, shown as a monthly time series.
options.analytical_goal: Measure and surface monthly changes in the proportion of assigned trainings completed so leaders can spot declines, improvements, or seasonal patterns and target interventions.

extracted_signals.label: terminal_metric
extracted_signals.value: Month-over-month training completion rate expressed as the percentage of assigned trainings completed within each calendar month (current and target values not provided).

extracted_signals.label: time_bound
extracted_signals.value: Last 12 months (rolling from report date) aggregated by calendar month, with instruction on whether to include the partial current month or restrict to full months.

extracted_signals.label: analysis_type
extracted_signals.value: Trend analysis showing month-to-month percentage-point change plus optional seasonality and year-over-year comparisons to contextualize fluctuations.

extracted_signals.label: data_requirement
extracted_signals.value: Cornerstone data including assignment records (assignment date, due date, assignment type), completion events (user id, completion timestamp), and learner employment status/organization hierarchy for scope filtering.

extracted_signals.label: cohort_definition
extracted_signals.value: Organization-wide scope should include active employees only (exclude terminated/inactive); optionally separate mandatory vs optional trainings if behavior differs.

extracted_signals.label: segmentation_scope
extracted_signals.value: Ability to break the MoM series by department, location, job level, or course to diagnose where changes originate and prioritize interventions.
